title: Regime-gated raw-categorical ensemble with sigmoid-only calibration
summary: Ordered boosting is well-suited for high-cardinality categorical interactions like Driver x Race x Compound, while sigmoid calibration preserves ranking unlike isotonic.
rationale: Ordered boosting is well-suited for high-cardinality categorical interactions like Driver x Race x Compound, while sigmoid calibration preserves ranking unlike isotonic. A gated blend can let different specialists dominate in slick, wet, early-race, and late-race regimes without damaging AUC.
implementation_hint: Train complementary specialists such as CatBoost on raw categories plus a tree model on engineered numerics, then learn simple regime-dependent blend weights from OOF predictions. If calibrating, prefer Platt/sigmoid per regime; avoid isotonic unless tie-induced AUC loss is acceptable.
expected_effect: Likely modest but reliable gain from better handling unseen/high-cardinality categories and distribution shifts across compounds/weather while preserving rank quality.
risk: Too many gating rules can overfit; keep regime definitions coarse and estimate weights strictly OOF.
